sourceRef: tip:Three common Chapter 33 mistakes to avoid

slides.headline: Avoid these rework traps
slides.body: Learn to spot errors early and ensure student veteran education benefits.

slides.headline: Incorrect COE
slides.body: VASCOs often mistake an outdated COE for a current one, leading to delays.
bullets: Verify COE authenticity with VASCO portal.

slides.headline: Chapter 33 22-1999
slides.body: Mistakes in the 22-1999 form can trigger rework. Ensure all fields are correctly filled.
bullets: Verify all required fields are filled.

slides.headline: Chapter 33 85/15
slides.body: Misinterpreting the 85/15 rule can lead to rework. Always refer to the current regulation.
bullets: Refer to the most recent VA guidance.
Stay updated with the latest regulations.

slides.headline: Misleading Phrases
slides.body: Vague or incorrect phrases in Chapter 33 can lead to confusion. Always use precise language.
bullets: Avoid terms like 'as soon as possible' without a clear timeline.
Specify dates and times for clarity.

slides.headline: Incorrect Timelines
slides.body: Using outdated or incorrect timelines can lead to rework. Always verify with the most current sources.
bullets: Refer to the latest VA guidelines for deadlines.
Cross-check with the VA website for updates.

slides.body: Join our waitlist for more tips and updates on Chapter 33.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Learn to avoid these common Chapter 33 mistakes and ensure compliance. Stay ahead of rework traps and keep student veteran education benefits on track. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is the third one, which could be more visually engaging with a clearer image or icon to represent the topic of 'Misleading Phrases'.
